Poland would win the hosting rights, gaining the most votes of the three.[[5]](#cite_note-auto-5)

**The 2016 [EHF](/wiki/European_Handball_Federation "European Handball Federation") [European Men's Handball Championship](/wiki/European_Men%27s_Handball_Championship "European Men's Handball Championship")** was the twelfth edition and was held for the first time in [Poland](/wiki/Poland "Poland") from 15–31 January 2016. [Croatia](/wiki/Croatia "Croatia") and [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway") were the other applicants in the bidding process.

[Poland](/wiki/Poland "Poland") was awarded the championship on the [EHF](/wiki/European_Handball_Federation "European Handball Federation") Congress in Monaco on 23 June 2012 with 58% votes.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Germany won their second title by beating Spain 24–17 in the final.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Croatia captured the bronze medal after defeating Norway 31–24.[[4]](#cite_note-4)
